| | | About Blue Amp Action: We cannot sugarcoat the current situation. Right-wing authoritarianism, defeated by a worldwide coalition during WWII, was only in hibernation. Over the past decade it has infected democratic societies--including ours. It is as simple as it is terrifying: What we do now will define the future of our country and world. We at Blue Amp Action are dedicated to liberal, open societies, where reason and science are applied to solve our problems; where civil rights--including voting rights!--are protected for all. We fight for these values every day. Speaking up on our platforms, raising the voices and the political prospects of liberal pols and pundits. **And savvy grassroots activists like you know, just like we do, that we can’t stop watching the people we help elect:_ after the elections, we have to keep organizing support for the policies we like and pushing back against the ones we don’t._** **__When the public tunes out between elections and the only people our elected officials hear from are the paid lobbyists of wealthy corporations, our government stops being accountable to the needs of ordinary families.__** **Democracy never sleeps, and neither can we.** So we ask that you support our work, so we can continue to engage in this fight every moment of every day. So our kids grow up in democracy, not dystopia. Thank you for your support for all we do! Please follow Cliff Schecter on Twitter @cliffschecter, or subscribe to The Takedown with Cliff Schecter on YouTube, to keep up to date with this battle for the heart and soul of our country. — _You're getting this message because you’ve supported good causes and Democratic candidates. We need your support to continue fighting for our democracy. We love having you in our network, but if you no longer wish to hear from us, you can[unsubscribe here](https://default.salsalabs.org/T3759f30e-6857-4fcf-a7f1-ca04f5858fed/368d17ff-e836-4168-8049-c5f0900fbbf9)._

Play the long game with your retirement... just as Trump plans to do with the economy.

**Trump's Win: Your Financial Game Plan for the Next 4 Years**
| | Donald Trump's return is a cause for celebration, but [there's something important you must remember](http://links.reclamingamericanfreedom.com/u/click?_t=5ec072595b1347f09b4bac0de82ea87d&_m=e713c949c5aa4e91aeeb4cc46cfc0c49&_e=zZ_i8m0RC-cvdBTvtE3lk0b9y8a3z0SodKsTIVfiULWycguy0JRwBKo83B5_n_Js7N_0rtFvoKpL6w4BZWr9ASqZTASt9TlprmM67pASwxnEyjDwFzgx68YNVWQuK06SkzcyIGHDfOhxACNktuFHYp_u1z5qtkjyBIzNKnsJJVowoPTgW1Ze6pzFSSGrxTwFxNNDnFUOY3TayVDPuKoIzc2Qx0HC8nAUgzf02LlDMCLfUwZ4LwV75N8QJIZFCSp2uwQ7GFkffgSuN5ppk1UW5tjGJXg4r_MMVj5whvRF1TZOYYE0HfuTqFbAsZhHbFcBNuuw4Y8gEmknUqZmtZJ4awsLobJqjIJbaku9LReZMBHFqa_K8tgb4MP8MsuUIAcyo9XQCeTDEgNAOYA7a9AsvQ%3D%3D). The road to economic recovery will be long and challenging. Why? Because President-elect Trump inherits a nation facing **significant economic problems:** 1\. A national debt exceeding $35 trillion and still growing
2\. Inflation that has eroded a chunk of the dollar's purchasing power
3\. A weakened American position on the global stage
4\. Growing support for socialism among young people While Trump's policies aim to address these issues, change won't happen overnight. In fact, **it might take _decades_ to reverse the damage**. Which means now, more than ever, it's critical for your savings to be diversified so that you can weather the ups and downs of economic recovery. This is why savvy Americans are turning to precious metals. And a Gold IRA can protect your IRA or 401(k) in a **tax-sheltered account** , regardless of market fluctuations or policy changes.

name: Hillsdale College via Conservatives Give
subject: The study of God
body:
One of the chief aims of education at Hillsdale College is to understand what kind of being we are, as well as our purpose.

This sponsored message from Conservatives Give is on behalf of Hillsdale College
[](https://links.conservativesgive.com/u/click?_t=959d0fa54cad47baa4213176314ae733&_m=c5f0c4ce27db448e9f5b31b3ed6a11b0&_e=--Vc-neCXKbJGtytuLBGbmx8_2czg8GE5ZXDEbf4ty_Dt41xwG7Mws1s8ja8fM3CNonjqAEgfCYvIDAMovdIQWPOhBpYEnkV9mGatSi0h7Tcg1HTuOT00FCoYKJ_nwO-Dqn7y_KmDnq8gAtCcQi0Ip1UKG777x35qakoD-MZfSkacDkGAtx7K27myofvhzrp8-d1EwnKnO4hV76J1Ka53K7I6PmXFvg09evtxZL_z_7mrmpPZzdZUqOY1vcClXXql9MuGvV7DbOq5eK1iapW-JT7HrNxLrvaam-j5wo17wKy3O0F4Em8oYhOnApwVRb-vc_g9SX1n2X6oo06rzRpl9QsjbR5qUiU8pMYiSnyVwHkqZuU86bfRIopqnJBN9XQIo3TejBODUlP9Ik5-X6_LNWKIj69f5LjHbp7183HE0Aoz0j1IDdmDpVG5QVEqIMU) Dear Fellow American, I hope your day is going well. I’m writing to let you know about something I think you’ll find useful in your learning. Reason and speech are gifts from God that enable us to think about God and to worship Him. [](https://links.conservativesgive.com/u/click?_t=959d0fa54cad47baa4213176314ae733&_m=c5f0c4ce27db448e9f5b31b3ed6a11b0&_e=--Vc-neCXKbJGtytuLBGbmx8_2czg8GE5ZXDEbf4ty_Dt41xwG7Mws1s8ja8fM3CNonjqAEgfCYvIDAMovdIQWPOhBpYEnkV9mGatSi0h7Tcg1HTuOT00FCoYKJ_nwO-Dqn7y_KmDnq8gAtCcQi0Ip1UKG777x35qakoD-MZfSkacDkGAtx7K27myofvhzrp8-d1EwnKnO4hV76J1Ka53K7I6PmXFvg09evtxZL_z_7mrmpPZzdZUqOY1vcClXXql9MuGvV7DbOq5eK1iapW-JT7HrNxLrvaam-j5wo17wLDUxrSLJVYB0sfVXdZ7-5zqDUYJlJk3i-ZA17XfE1zODfgYv-X6eU-H1UzTubB8WPN9C94oWNvN7NIOxr-P92qRqE9slFjriA32-oYeC4tN6uSPQOsS9onT1dBYf57Jsuik3Ys3x1C0gemTzMg0Tgw) One of the chief aims of education at Hillsdale College is to understand what kind of being we are, as well as our purpose. That, in turn, makes it possible to begin to understand our relationship to God.

The study of God, also known as “theology,” has had a profound impact on Western civilization, stretching back thousands of years to the ancient Hebrews. And that’s what Hillsdale’s **“Theology 101”** online course is about—the development of theology from the Old Testament through the twentieth century.

**“Theology 101”** has been a popular course, so I’m pleased to let you know that it is [**now available for you to own on DVD**](https://links.conservativesgive.com/u/click?_t=959d0fa54cad47baa4213176314ae733&_m=c5f0c4ce27db448e9f5b31b3ed6a11b0&_e=--Vc-neCXKbJGtytuLBGbmx8_2czg8GE5ZXDEbf4ty_Dt41xwG7Mws1s8ja8fM3CNonjqAEgfCYvIDAMovdIQWPOhBpYEnkV9mGatSi0h7Tcg1HTuOT00FCoYKJ_nwO-Dqn7y_KmDnq8gAtCcQi0Ip1UKG777x35qakoD-MZfSkacDkGAtx7K27myofvhzrp8-d1EwnKnO4hV76J1Ka53K7I6PmXFvg09evtxZL_z_7mrmpPZzdZUqOY1vcClXXql9MuGvV7DbOq5eK1iapW-JT7HrNxLrvaam-j5wo17wLA5KPRR7eihEKWF2PYE0b8pk6kLJdFo7NfJVBldcdpDixIVVTrXgh4K6YBuZ5fyoBwpA62WaGzj767sK7T6DrZWNUx-YH1j0tclyQQY3ZfpkcB4I6Wcg_Dtip3Vuld7o3m07wghofmc8MWd7NxWjdk). You can add it to your home library or give it as a gift to a friend or relative.

We do have a limited number of sets in stock, though—and they go fast—so don’t delay in getting your copy of **“Theology 101”** on DVD. Secure yours right now at: **[https://secured.hillsdale.edu/support-hillsdales-online-courses-theology-101-dvd](https://links.conservativesgive.com/u/click?_t=959d0fa54cad47baa4213176314ae733&_m=c5f0c4ce27db448e9f5b31b3ed6a11b0&_e=--Vc-neCXKbJGtytuLBGbmx8_2czg8GE5ZXDEbf4ty_Dt41xwG7Mws1s8ja8fM3CNonjqAEgfCYvIDAMovdIQWPOhBpYEnkV9mGatSi0h7Tcg1HTuOT00FCoYKJ_nwO-Dqn7y_KmDnq8gAtCcQi0Ip1UKG777x35qakoD-MZfSkacDkGAtx7K27myofvhzrp8-d1EwnKnO4hV76J1Ka53K7I6PmXFvg09evtxZL_z_7mrmpPZzdZUqOY1vcClXXql9MuGvV7DbOq5eK1iapW-JT7HrNxLrvaam-j5wo17wK6W2jHbm0Ztvg_uPY2RTqw1oynRLLfx1BTgXIpeBdAxbIw8CBFyA0zJDxaqv67Rxjj7S5OpFKOPBCNkGIWy9hDLk7ADca6tFIn4SIQcq3QCllKoMDrlZRF2DOjRWYfyrIjwzws65QO4OsOpE0NupC2)** Best regards,

Jeremiah Regan, Ph.D.
Executive Director of Online Learning
Hillsdale College

---
# Trump’s Plan To Avoid
# The Elites Raid On Your 401(k)
---
Donald Trump has reclaimed the White House, and America is on the path to victory once again.

But don’t think for a second that the Dems on the inside will go down without a fight.

Whether stirring up controversy through identity politics in the liberal media, or creating economic turbulence through market manipulation, there are many present threats to your financial future.

They use these tactics to enrich themselves while everyday Americans watch their retirement accounts slowly erode under market volatility.

The constant volatility benefits them, but it’s draining the hard-earned money in your retirement accounts.

Whether it’s a 401(k), IRA, TSP, or pension, your post retirement future could be in serious danger if you are not aware of the options you have to protect your wealth from their game.
[Click Here for Your FREE Wealth Protection Guide](http://e.goppresidential.com/rd/9z2zrfu4p59bujsot66qeaqkh1m0apgmk87hfgagn38_rp22sh2s8i6epj2c1j64p76cph2bs)
The good news is, Trump is fighting for you!

Thanks to a crucial IRS loophole that President Trump kept open, you can transfer your funds tax-free and penalty-free to a safer, more reliable option that isn’t tied to the manipulated stock market.

Don’t wait until it’s too late.

Dems in the Senate and the House could make moves to close this loophole at any time, and once it’s gone, you’ll be back at the mercy of a rigged system.
[Download Your Free WEALTH PROTECTION GUIDE ](http://e.goppresidential.com/rd/9z2zsogp4b3bfcp1jtq3fbqu31apjpobs78kclebq68_rp22sh2s8i6epj2c1j64p76cph2bs)and secure your future before the deep state strikes again with another wave of orchestrated volatility.

| | Donald Trump's return is a cause for celebration, but [there's something important you must remember](https://links.defend-the-constitution.org/u/click?_t=959d0fa54cad47baa4213176314ae733&_m=4781bc09d9ea44e985d0178e1e3dd45e&_e=Zxvh5QJf5yuD8gFNQfT5eQlnY5OooD2qwwj3pOOotiMuDXsq8lUdYE5H861HF4HhV3RzfnyYa2kjzPai3cH0EBlLMeQn5JxPqdx0bQ56WYBv1YrFYVFbP0pM9r9V0r8UoV_cvU4fHaLJTD2nwAEtssSbBgLbbqeBrucVZRMXmQ73mbP07NMPMzjMbZxbl6rzaXiAexYZrWBGs4Nlb9iYfcww8pTH58uxfPBAfq5fWv80ccwsRuDJ0o0nwX60ypVTjK1g3CNPxE7WUs3ALOamUBAJqgYUXzGD4-ul0nSy6lRldvrTVLmxV6E_qFUXqr4oVCERyTFfL8Qze3OAkgddTlgAbskMrnRB_JAE27eV5Hlbv9SJ9hI0Ec--Br2iawQqpkBHxX7N2JjouyGjMfbFMQ%3D%3D). The road to economic recovery will be long and challenging. Why? Because President-elect Trump inherits a nation facing **significant economic problems:** 1\. A national debt exceeding $35 trillion and still growing
2\. Inflation that has eroded a chunk of the dollar's purchasing power
3\. A weakened American position on the global stage
4\. Growing support for socialism among young people While Trump's policies aim to address these issues, change won't happen overnight. In fact, **it might take _decades_ to reverse the damage**. Which means now, more than ever, it's critical for your savings to be diversified so that you can weather the ups and downs of economic recovery. This is why savvy Americans are turning to precious metals. And a Gold IRA can protect your IRA or 401(k) in a **tax-sheltered account** , regardless of market fluctuations or policy changes.

Turning Point USA is pleased to announce our newest content creator, Bo Alford, but you know him as bodittle! Bo is a bold Christian conservative who excels in “man-on-the-street” interviews, using sharp wit and humor to spark conversations and expose the contradictions in leftist ideologies. His raw, unscripted moments have already garnered millions of views for Turning Point USA in just the last few months! Whether he's being confronted at an LGBTQ parade for challenging radical transgender ideology, getting leftists to expose their own hypocrisy by signing prank petitions, or interviewing people at FuryCon, Bo never holds back.
---
[ ](https://d1K2-504.na1.hubspotlinks.com/Ctc/DN+113/d1K2-504/VVWcSH6bGg7MW2mKKXQ56Sc3xW6rg8CD5nBkgnN4CDgfC3m2ndW8wLKSR6lZ3lPW6t3GCV33Rn90N5JvfXy4ksmXW6MkTv231KZ3NVkyvcj9kxJ4fVWx-323GdN_gW1W0c8K5j_2QRW4cLDNf2r-Xg9W7NR0M28x1LNZW1SK2S94xf1l8W92ZKQp6_qSPFW12jFJs38NsybW5lZsv77ST6MTN3Ch71h7GNCzW5kWPsy1627xYW1Hcr-X6vMc9gW40msZK1MXlF_W7ZBXRX3vGzhWW7mHVky3pD6dxW916Yjk3nzGphW85Pw_M2H1kSwW85Vj7Y14wMbmW1mWxC26f_zcJW3tmx8w4kKTPjVYXRq630ZxGTW2D76JF6FRpcHW2Xs2_c3pMPzcW93blvY77yX9DW5cmjWV6MN3Jcf4nskFW04)
---
Bo uses his humor and creativity to captivate his audience, while also shedding light on the weaknesses of America’s broken culture. At 22 years old, he is all-in on defending conservative Christian values and building an unstoppable movement alongside Turning Point USA! As a Turning Point USA Content Creator, Bo can be seen on Turning Point USA’s social media platforms and at Turning Point USA events. clean_body: Bo is fearlessly tackling the culture war with his greatest weapon—his camera in hand. | Turning Point USA is pleased to announce our newest content creator, Bo Alford, but you know him as bodittle! Bo is a bold Christian conservative who excels in “man-on-the-street” interviews, using sharp wit and humor to spark conversations and expose the contradictions in leftist ideologies. His raw, unscripted moments have already garnered millions of views for Turning Point USA in just the last few months! Whether he's being confronted at an LGBTQ parade for challenging radical transgender ideology, getting leftists to expose their own hypocrisy by signing prank petitions, or interviewing people at FuryCon, Bo never holds back. Bo uses his humor and creativity to captivate his audience, while also shedding light on the weaknesses of America’s broken culture. At 22 years old, he is all-in on defending conservative Christian values and building an unstoppable movement alongside Turning Point USA! As a Turning Point USA Content Creator, Bo can be seen on Turning Point USA’s social media platforms and at Turning Point USA events. Bo will also be covering top news stories and protests for TPUSA Frontlines. You can support Bo by following him on , , and . | Turning Point USA, 4940 East Beverly Road, Phoenix, AZ 85044, United States of America, (844) 872-1776

**Good morning!** On this day in 1854, Sam Houston joined Washington County’s Independence Baptist Church and was [baptized in nearby Little Rocky Creek](http://email.editor.thetexasflyover.com/c/eJx0z7GurCAQxvGngU7DjANqQXEbu73tKc24oJKgbJCs69ufuP2p_tX3S76fx_jgEMO-_OfN2_KZ04gKqQKooB8dh3iNJJ2dlOtBegttgwp012m5Ws2z0lq1_OyMYSDPYNjoqTXITEQy2BsDgB6gAaS614CdmaEhP02EWpDyLpSU67L64j98zPFKb5_rZ9pktGspr0M0_wQOAofzPOtyrJz2GHZfp7wIHL6ryvFVTdcdgYPfS76rG5mte50hxnCYVpBaNg7xa5c_z74t_gYAAP__igNV1A) at the urging of his wife and her mother. The pew where Houston routinely sat has been preserved and marked. SpaceX is launching its Starship rocket today at 4 p.m. for its sixth test flight. ## Aggies Remember Bonfire Tragedy 25 Years Later
Thousands of Texas A&M students [held a remembrance ceremony](http://email.editor.thetexasflyover.com/c/eJx0kLGOgzAQBb_GdEbsYkMoXFxDl2uvRAavg6U1jmxfSP7-lPTXvzcazc91udrA4bh920imPn1asEMlASRMi7OBX4tqnFk7N0FDBsYeO9CXi252QxPZtQMLqMn1wwT9Zj15p70aPW5jE8wbBgATQA-o2kkDXgYPvaJ1VaiF6siFmnJbd6r0tMXzKz0ot1uKDZu91nsR_ZfAWeB8nmfr0xOHPf2Wmo73SOB80FkEzp-7tFGu6fAhk9wSs70Xkqjli2wukm2lLDNFiivlcNwkoOTwoCI5ldpk4-5nYA5lGIXqbtEG_pjUf9M8DP4FAAD__xqGaEA) early Monday morning to honor the victims of the fatal bonfire collapse that occurred 25 years ago, ahead of that year’s football game against the University of Texas. [Twelve people were killed and 27 injured](http://email.editor.thetexasflyover.com/c/eJx0kLGOpDAQRL_GZI3ctoEhcDAXkO2lF6L2ugFLNoyMZxn-_gT5Rt2qJz2V6t_X-EUhhnX-S4lt-UzbqKQygAjYj55CPEdTeeuk77Fii51WEpvHo6kW69EZ6ZzrO-61lo46rVppWt17xzi1VbCXDBF7RI3K1H2D6tFOqA07Z1QjjGQfypbrsnDhD-1TPLcfzvX3lqpol1Jeu9BPoQahhuM46rSvFxNq4BXeu1DDysd17l81cDLlHWgqnKEsDLcVCBK4bZ1CZvjeYqTXzjemeQ5XlNJ7DeWEzImT40sBnsnHE0qmmf0p1EAZnk98T-0fqrL1ryPEGPa2E0bOiUK8W5dfZ_yx6n8AAAD__yA3df8) when the stack of 5,000 or so logs collapsed at 2:42 a.m. on Thursday, Nov. 18, 1999. Although the annual tradition dates back to the early 1900s and was organized in part by school administrators, subsequent bonfires have been held exclusively off-campus. A memorial to those who died and were wounded was unveiled in 2004. This year’s student bonfire is [scheduled for next Friday](http://email.editor.thetexasflyover.com/c/eJx0kLGOgzAQBb_GdEasbcAULq6hy7VXooVdJ5YMjrAvwN-fkv7692ak-blNNwwxbPdvXNmV06dJNcpIAAnDRBjiNZmK3NzQABU76LVqoLW2rR7OGka_6N52VmnUXtuuWQZaDFmy1FEV3BsGAAOABmXqoQVlOw_a8Dwb1QrTMIWS9ro8uPCJ2ccrvXivl7RW0T1KeWahv4QahRqP46h9Onv8zSVs74lQ48ZHFmr8nCWukhgpXnJOmw87yyXFiM_MErctvHjPuF_V7uh5hBhD7nphmvuKIX6M5d8EL6f-AgAA__9Qd1_n), the day before the Aggies play the Longhorns for the first time since Texas A&M joined the SEC in 2012.
## Texas Students Among Winners in National STEM Competition
Six Texas middle schoolers were [among the 30 finalists](http://email.editor.thetexasflyover.com/c/eJx00LFugzAQgOGnMZuR7zAhDB66sKVrR2Tsc7jKYGS7IXn7Kt37Ar8-_V-3-WY58n7_tBuZ-gxpRoVaAkgYZ285vmbdeLMoP0JDBoYOFfTXa9-sJoDr1RK0G6xXC_VhROVh8FYRIQbXsHnHAGAE6AB1O_aA10uATtOyaOyFVuS5ptzWlSo9bQnxlR6UW5e2Jpq11qOI7kPgJHA6z7MtyTHVV0i5OKbdUZvyXeB0ZCpFZopkCwmc6kp5SzJwWSnLb3aypkN2Stp9Tz-7o432Kt88gVOTjT9OjpHLZRBa3TfL8Y9Q_33yMPgbAAD__3GtZVc) in a national STEM competition organized by Thermo Fisher Scientific, a leading supplier of classroom instruments. Two of those students, 14-year-old Samvith Mahadevan of Round Rock and 14-year-old Gary Allen Montelongo of La Joya, were named top winners of the contest, which had over 2,000 entrants. Mahadevan developed a device that incorporates an AI algorithm to [detect allergens in food products](http://email.editor.thetexasflyover.com/c/eJx0kLFuwyAUAL8Gb1i8Z2yHgaGLt3TtaD0bSFAwREBC8vdVunc-6U66n_N6Jh98vHzTYXV9ubSiQMkBOKjVkA_vVXZGb8Io6KyGeUAB4-k0dldtUE27mGlWckScEN3s3GbEDg4mtNR5_ZEBgAIYAGWvRsDT5GCQdtskjkwKa3xNua9XW-2Ligvv9LS539PRBX2t9V7Y8MVwYbi01vrbi-IHMlyibYXhktMjGp7TfuO-GF7qw9hYefOx8EjVp0iBl2oPTo2yYbh0WZt78yH4Ms1MistBPvwV678Lnhp_AwAA___fUF7c), citing his own experience with food allergies as the inspiration for creating the tool. Montelongo studied how train car suspension springs and weight distribution [affect train derailments](http://email.editor.thetexasflyover.com/c/eJx00DGvozAQBOBfY7og79rGuHCBdOKqXHslMrCQPTk4wlYIzf32p0f_umlm9Gn-3od74Mjb-ic8yZfPkgaUqG8AN3DDHDieg65mP8rZQUUerEIJpm1N9fAwm5EQAACtbWyrgSbnpG0b66QxumL_PQYADkAB6toZwLZZQGkaR41GaEkzl7TX5UGFPiEv8Uxv2uspPavoH6W8slCdwF5gfxxHndPEVM4l7Xli2iaq074K7P_xJLC_7AtvIXIu-Wopobr_QnWFPkWoX7_Dfgo0KLsYabvSPW2FYtrWVO1-fh0cI-fGCi3XZ-B4UcqP37w9fgUAAP__a2VjLg), using his coding knowledge to create a program that measures vibrations in the springs of model trains. Both students [received $10,000](http://email.editor.thetexasflyover.com/c/eJx00DGOpDAQQNHTmKwQZQw0gYNNyHrTDZHB5aZWBiO71DS3H83kc4Gvr_fvOT8dRz5ef91OVj4hzbrRBhABx9k7jvdsKm-Xxo9YkcWh1Q12j0dXbdYYQ7rt-r4NrhnCGlwYhsX3fjFmGMNSsf2OIeKI2KI29dihfvQBW0PLYnSnTEOeJeVaNhL6uBLind6U6zXtVbSbyFlU-0fpSenpuq66pJVJ7pByWZmOleqUX0pPZ6ZSIFMkV0jpCVu4yWVI0YOnN8V0FricUIbAUbITTgeUuwjtcPFRQDbKe4LAZaMM_3kFSSe4y2Wv9FRl68-LY-TSD8o0r91x_LmUX9neVn8FAAD__5t9c0g), and their schools received $1,000 each to support STEM learning.
## Work Starts on Trinity River Park
A _Dallas Morning News_ columnist reflected on the significance of [work starting on the $325 million Harold Simmons Park](http://email.editor.thetexasflyover.com/c/eJx0kDHvpCAUxD8Nds_4EHUtKLax2yvvSgMH6MsiGGDX9T79xe3_1Uwyk8nk9-cxPxR5CssvtVlZPi7OvOECEAHH2Sjy5ywqI3VjRqysxKHlDXa3W1etcsS-67uGazc4pThqYRrVu1YPo3bC2orkNYaII2KLXNRjh_zWO2yF1VrwjonGGiox1WW1xX5Udv6Mb5vqv3GrvFxL2TNr74xPjE_HcdRbDlfG-GQDvDLjU7DHJV9_xPSEGMAo71WGkihQOSHR2ybYVXqCo6C8P0HbhUKGIyaTgaCs8bWsBQgMBHu1y7lbxieV4H7Hl_jt_1VJmv0g7yn3AxPNsiny36PlR3Jvyf8HAAD__-mpcmI), a 250-acre development along the Trinity River that many longtime Dallas residents doubted would ever come to fruition. Sharon Grigsby, who has written for the newspaper since 1980, recounted the numerous ideas proposed over the last few decades for developing the Trinity River, from a barge canal to an urban lake to a tollway. The park, funded in part by a [$50 million donation](http://email.editor.thetexasflyover.com/c/eJx0kL2OszAQAJ_GdEbexYRQuPgaunztlWgTL8G65Uf25oe3P-X660cz0nxdxgslSev9Py0c9D1tIzr0FsBCP0ZKcoy-iuHqYg8VB-gadNCez201Bz617NDhBC1T17FzzlMTfe8Yp67hKoWPDAB6gAbQ130LeD5N0Hi-Xj22xjuOSbdc68zKbyqTHNuTc33blkrCrLoX0_wzOBgcIolQqW8P0UfmhfYPZXBY-VUMDplJLBclZYOD5rQmPeyUnpztTHmTaEtalm0tdqf8bXCocoj7K4mkcuqMd_eFkvym9c8Xz4A_AQAA__87fWIJ) from the widow of the late Dallas businessman Harold Simmons, will feature skyline overlooks, skate parks, splash pads, roller rinks, gardens, trails, and kayaking facilities. Harold Simmons Park is [projected to create 530 jobs](http://email.editor.thetexasflyover.com/c/eJx0kLFyhCAURb8GOxzeExULijR2mzalgwLrmyDsAFl3_z5j-nS3uHPOzPm6LTdDgeL90xxO15dPCwqUHIDDtFhD4b3IxupV2Akap2HsUECvVN_seuyHcfNKglDTuA7TIITzsCmh0HvVy4b0BQOACaADlO3UA6rBQyfdukrsmRTOUk25rbur7mWKD-_0dLnd0tEEvdf6KKz7YDgznM_zbH16yejOch0YztdkOG8plpp_tkop8tXdKRa-m5yC5YWOI8XCHyZ_c2tCMIXXTJHqm2d6utxkbR8nhUBlGJkU98NQ-PPXf4M8Nf4GAAD__y1CZKg) and attract 4 to 6 million annual visitors. Construction is expected to take about three years to complete.

clean_body: Thousands of Texas A&M students held a remembrance ceremony early Monday morning to honor the victims of the fatal bonfire collapse that occurred 25 years ago, ahead of that year’s football game against the University of Texas. | | | | | | Tuesday, November 19, 2024 ---|--- Good morning! On this day in 1854, Sam Houston joined Washington County’s Independence Baptist Church and was at the urging of his wife and her mother. The pew where Houston routinely sat has been preserved and marked. SpaceX is launching its Starship rocket today at 4 p.m. for its sixth test flight. You can . Do you love Texas? So do our 230,000+ readers. Advertise in The Texas Flyover and connect your business with passionate locals. . We're sponsored today by our friends at . Be sure to check them out in today's edition. Abilene ☀️ 72/48° | Amarillo ☀️ 56/33° | Austin ☀️ 80/50° | Brownsville 🌤️ 87/62° | Corpus Christi ☀️ 86/56° | DFW ☀️ 73/49° | El Paso ☀️ 63/38° | Houston ☀️ 78/56° | Lubbock ☀️ 64/41° | San Antonio ☀️ 81/51° | Texarkana ☀️ 74/50° | Aggies Remember Bonfire Tragedy 25 Years Later Thousands of Texas A&M students early Monday morning to honor the victims of the fatal bonfire collapse that occurred 25 years ago, ahead of that year’s football game against the University of Texas. when the stack of 5,000 or so logs collapsed at 2:42 a.m. on Thursday, Nov. 18, 1999. Although the annual tradition dates back to the early 1900s and was organized in part by school administrators, subsequent bonfires have been held exclusively off-campus. A memorial to those who died and were wounded was unveiled in 2004. This year’s student bonfire is , the day before the Aggies play the Longhorns for the first time since Texas A&M joined the SEC in 2012. Texas Students Among Winners in National STEM Competition Six Texas middle schoolers were in a national STEM competition organized by Thermo Fisher Scientific, a leading supplier of classroom instruments. Two of those students, 14-year-old Samvith Mahadevan of Round Rock and 14-year-old Gary Allen Montelongo of La Joya, were named top winners of the contest, which had over 2,000 entrants. Mahadevan developed a device that incorporates an AI algorithm to , citing his own experience with food allergies as the inspiration for creating the tool. Montelongo studied how train car suspension springs and weight distribution , using his coding knowledge to create a program that measures vibrations in the springs of model trains. Both students , and their schools received $1,000 each to support STEM learning. Work Starts on Trinity River Park A Dallas Morning News columnist reflected on the significance of , a 250-acre development along the Trinity River that many longtime Dallas residents doubted would ever come to fruition. Sharon Grigsby, who has written for the newspaper since 1980, recounted the numerous ideas proposed over the last few decades for developing the Trinity River, from a barge canal to an urban lake to a tollway. The park, funded in part by a from the widow of the late Dallas businessman Harold Simmons, will feature skyline overlooks, skate parks, splash pads, roller rinks, gardens, trails, and kayaking facilities. Harold Simmons Park is and attract 4 to 6 million annual visitors. Construction is expected to take about three years to complete. | Ibuprofen 😳😜 Stomach pain, dizziness, heart concerns, kidney problems... These are just some of the scary side effects that can be found on the back of a bottle of Ibuprofen. And to top it all off, Ibuprofen doesn’t even address the main cause of your problem... It’s only temporary pain relief. You don't need to just alleviate your pain–you need to fight INFLAMMATION. That’s why researchers speculate could outperform Ibuprofen... And it doesn’t have the dangerous side effects mentioned above either! == > | ➤ Little Elm: A resident recorded a purported mountain lion "screaming very aggressively” early Thursday morning. Several mountain lion sightings have been reported in Collin County over the past few weeks. () ➤ Cases of whooping cough, or pertussis, have tripled in Texas since last year, according to the Department of State Health Services. The uptick aligns with data showing a five-fold spike in cases across the country. () ➤ Liberty County: Hardin Middle School Principal Chad Barrett, 56, was arrested after investigators found child pornography on his cell phone. Barrett was removed from school grounds and placed on administrative leave. () ➤ Lubbock: Gail and Bill Roberts, owners of Galaxy Pawn since 1995, are closing after more than two years of road construction has led to a steep decline in business. () ➤ Dallas: Amaya Medrano, 19, allegedly shot and killed a 66-year-old man at an Oak Cliff gas station on Friday after meeting him to purchase a phone advertised on Facebook Marketplace. Medrano has been charged with murder and is being held on a $1 million bond. () ➤ Laredo: Thieves hijacked two trucks transporting over 24,000 bottles of tequila worth about $1 million. The tequila business is owned by celebrity chef Guy Fieri and rock-and-roll singer Sammy Hagar. “It’s like a movie, but it’s real,” Fieri said. name: The Flyover
subject: Trump Confirms Deportation Plan, Holiday Displays, and 320 Tarantulas on a Plane
body:
President-elect Donald [Trump confirmed on Monday](http://email.editor.jointheflyover.com/c/eJx0kLtuhDAUBb_GdEZ-gXHhIlJEt1G6lMjYF_ZGfiDj7Iq_jzZ96qMzGs3Xbbk5jJj3D5fAtq0sgglFOafcLMFhvBbVBbsBCJAdWK6lYHyU09jdrRmM0Mq5SYEx2zp4oU0wYdWDl5MA3aF90TjnhnNpFO9h1GxzmnFm2LQCJ4pBwFZq_10wtzts8SoPqL0vqYv23tpxEvlGxEzE7Faf4Xn2e3nNRMyfJWJDfxIxt_qTDupL3rCmkx7RZRrAR1eBZtewZBcpJKg7ZH_RhBGbqxdN7nzdz1bqReSMgch3zgczSqWmrtpwPDFGPEdNFNuTw_in1v5L9bDiNwAA___DEmvN) that he would declare a national emergency to fulfill his campaign promise of mass deportations of illegal migrants living in the U.S.

| | |
---
| [](http://email.editor.jointheflyover.com/c/eJx0j71uhDAQhJ_G7kC7tvkrXESJ6C5tSuTD62Mjg09gEfH2ka-_ema-mfm5TTfHkbfHt1vJ5pAmBcpUiBUOk3ccr8lIbwORIi3JYqcVYKv7Vi52Dib0qtVgZrjPvQfXNHNQg0fAJrhesi00RBwQ9WCwpraD4DpAGKC_EwoD5Dmnvf5NvOWFQrzSSXs9p1VGu-T8PIT-EGoUauTtpCPXC8WY9uugYiqKHvPmVjqE_ipZoT5f86vSLXfrn38cIx9tJww8VsfxRc_v3p5W_QcAAP__jl9Ulg) | |
# Tuesday, November 19, 2024
---|---
**Good Morning**! On this day in 1863, President Abraham Lincoln delivered the [Gettysburg Address](http://email.editor.jointheflyover.com/c/eJx00LFuwyAUheGngQ2LCxTsgaGLt3TtaOFwbd-KmAioLb99lapr5086R_o_b9MtUKJ9_QgP9G3Jk5LKCAABwxQDpWsyPPoFUaHm6MFpJcHq3vLN6xjxrTcAw2L7IAHBzDYG7KV2DvTMyb_WAGAA0IOBDq2TS3AS5CD7GYEZiZFaLt1Xpr1tuKQrH1i6e37w5LfWnpXpd6ZGpsbzPLuNasvlejlTY9uoihguQbv4E6bGRPs9p11ETHRgqWLF1q46f5dVhBgL1sqLj8-TUqJqHTNyfQRKv6ftvwiHVz8BAAD__6HDYEo), one of the most memorable speeches in American history. In fewer than 275 words, Lincoln reminded a war-weary public why the Union needed to fight and win the Civil War. If you haven’t put up your Christmas tree yet, [check out today’s item](http://email.editor.jointheflyover.com/c/eJx0kLFuhSAUQL8GN8y9wBMdGLq4va4dDZWr3gbFANG8v29e9-4n5yTn6zk9PUc-1k-_k6tLmhQoIxElDlPwHF-TaYJbiBTphhxarQA73XfN5ixQ3y-mUxoeytqwDAEBHw_dWYMe-obd24aIA6IeDLbUWVi8BYQB-m9CYYAC15Tbn8RH3WiJr3RRbue0N9FttZ5F6A-hRqHG-77byBcfK9X5TQg1-nDxTEKNwV8cZPR3SYect8yl7r7ImolkoDllX_lYZeWzNNmF8-YYuXRWGFh3z_GvWP87cDn1GwAA__-XZ13u) for expert tips from a pro on crafting a stunning tree. Our favorite tip? “ _Have fun with it!_ " **_The Tennessee Flyover,_** our newest state publication, launched today, and we invite all our Volunteer State readers to give it a try. You can [sign up automatically by clicking here.](http://email.editor.jointheflyover.com/c/eJx0kDFvwyAUhH8NbIl4gMEeGCpFXqpkqlR1srB52K_FJrJpqvz7ymqHDO14p--74V7P3dlTomW8-BldibmTQuoDwAGaLnhK907z4CKiRMXRgVVSgFG14ZOLoRdBaFEPutLeQhWtl721fd-YOFQ1J7evAUADoBoNRzRWRG8FiEbUPQLTAgOVvB7fMy1lwpju-YbrccgzT24q5box9cRky2Q7pz8oJtvftBN-pCHR8sFUi7OnxNQpXL8oJdqMZbLSYtzrH9FsxRfshhyQqdPLhUnzWWamTs9vfHUP3oPFy38n3Zz8DgAA___8yGjX) Invest in the company that won over the Dragons with today's sponsor [RYSE](http://email.editor.jointheflyover.com/c/eJx0j71uhDAQhJ_G7kC7tvkrXESJ6C5tSuTD62Mjg09gEfH2ka-_ema-mfm5TTfHkbfHt1vJ5pAmBcpUiBUOk3ccr8lIbwORIi3JYqcVYKv7Vi52Dib0qtVgZrjPvQfXNHNQg0fAJrhesi00RBwQ9WCwpraD4DpAGKC_EwoD5Dmnvf5NvOWFQrzSSXs9p1VGu-T8PIT-EGoUauTtpCPXC8WY9uugYiqKHvPmVjqE_ipZoT5f86vSLXfrn38cIx9tJww8VsfxRc_v3p5W_QcAAP__jl9Ulg).
|
---
## Trump Confirms Deportation Plan
President-elect Donald [Trump confirmed on Monday](http://email.editor.jointheflyover.com/c/eJx0kLtuhDAUBb_GdEZ-gXHhIlJEt1G6lMjYF_ZGfiDj7Iq_jzZ96qMzGs3Xbbk5jJj3D5fAtq0sgglFOafcLMFhvBbVBbsBCJAdWK6lYHyU09jdrRmM0Mq5SYEx2zp4oU0wYdWDl5MA3aF90TjnhnNpFO9h1GxzmnFm2LQCJ4pBwFZq_10wtzts8SoPqL0vqYv23tpxEvlGxEzE7Faf4Xn2e3nNRMyfJWJDfxIxt_qTDupL3rCmkx7RZRrAR1eBZtewZBcpJKg7ZH_RhBGbqxdN7nzdz1bqReSMgch3zgczSqWmrtpwPDFGPEdNFNuTw_in1v5L9bDiNwAA___DEmvN) that he would declare a national emergency to fulfill his campaign promise of mass deportations of illegal migrants living in the U.S. Trump made the statement on Truth Social, [responding "TRUE!!!"](http://email.editor.jointheflyover.com/c/eJx0kL_uozAQhJ_GdI68hoApXFxDl2uvRA5ekz35D7I3iXj7E9f_milmpE-j789jfTiKlPffLqHlUFat9CABJMyrdxTPdei8DYga-w4tTL1WMPZm7F52xjmMs3d66A087-AMbE8wdxPuxk-D6cheNACYAfp5gBuOkwpuUqBmZZ4IYlDoiUu9_S2U-YUhnuWD9baV1EX7Yj6a6H8JvQi95PMoja9J6OUCC70AXGGEXt5NZvw2oReu73TIreRANTV5RJeb5CLfDWWiSOzqKUOpG16tx6NUlon26jI36QJjlR636CrlXWbHVLKLEhPWHfN2Cr101frjSzFSGycxqD05iv9P808SP1b_CwAA__9G8HT4) to a post from Judicial Watch chief Tom Fitton's post about using military assets to reverse Biden's immigration policies. Advocates for illegal migrants are [preparing a legal defense](http://email.editor.jointheflyover.com/c/eJx0kL1q5DAUhZ9G7mR0Za9_ChWzLC4WJk0gSWeurWvPDfoxljJm8vTB06c6Bw58HL7363hFdhzWF_Rk8hJHrXQtAST0o0V2j7EurFmINFUFGWgrraCpuqa4GdXPtp_0Us_2T4dL3zSNntTUar10oGYs2Jw0AOgBqr6GkppWLdgqUL3qJgJRK7Kc415-Rg75Rot7xDvt5Rx94cwt5y2J6iL0IPRwHEfpUzg3oQcK8isJPQQ6znh29p7XHUOWaO9xxkxJ-jix42-SuCKHlOXmMASy0mNK0tIW94yZYzgBuMvLBfLHf_0mqiHObEX17--ph470SvtW7MZuBzvHqWlFrVaP7J5v82_27kb_BAAA__9bxnND) against mass deportations, with an ACLU official stating they are ready to challenge the efforts in court and, if necessary, on the streets. Alfonso Aguilar, a former U.S. Office of Citizenship official, stated that opponents of the policy were [responding to fearmongering](http://email.editor.jointheflyover.com/c/eJx0kDGPnDAQhX-N6WblMbAshYs9RXQXKUqREg14YOdkbGTMEfLrI66_akZ6eu_pfX_e-3cSL2H-SQvbPMXeaFMBImDbOxJ_9lXh7MRsuCzYYlMajffycS9ednroxlUtYV3VpLWhoUHTDFTXeqxdWxdirzREbBHLtsIb3xs9UaNRt_oxMKpKs5Mc0-0jSsgvnvwZPzndxrgU3r5yXjdVPpXplOmO47gtW7g0ZToOsG_KdIGP66zRS5bxenPalxUcj56ShBkCZYmBPPDCaeYwnkAZhpgcJzji7h2EmMEzOcgRFvGSKcm_LxvECca4h5xO4L8rpwwbnVcNJXg-cf-gX2-q7OIoTpU_3i6UfGy_Oa1Fsm49xHvZ7o2q9LyQ-K9l-TvSn9b8DwAA___pGIO7), emphasizing that initial deportations would focus on targeted arrests of criminals.
## Spirit Airlines Files for Bankruptcy Protection
[Spirit Airlines filed for Chapter 11](http://email.editor.jointheflyover.com/c/eJx0kLGO6yAQRb_G7ogYwBgKiteky2u3tAYzJLNLbAvYRPn7Vbbf8upKR0fn47JckAtv1_94p9DzviipjAAQ4JeEXF6LGVPIRIr0SAFmrSRY7ex4C04iaUtmUnqNSUYdk_SAMU8WpHEwcnjTAMADaG_gRHaWGWcJ0ksXCQYjKXHf6-lz563fKJfX_qB6Wvf7WMKt96MN-t-gzoM647HRs72v96id10KDOreDK3eBXAtv1ETE7at-H319iUSxi7K3Rk3MTq1zjs4rTBmSytpaAxAx-mmy1o01pOPJpXCz82Dk9Y5cfj36X10eQf0EAAD__1JOY7E) bankruptcy protection Monday after accumulating more than $2.5 billion in losses since 2020 and facing looming debt payments exceeding $1 billion. The filing follows [numerous business challenges](http://email.editor.jointheflyover.com/c/eJx00LGOtCAUBeCnwU7DBQawoPgbu_nbLQ3iZby7KAbZMb79xu23Oc1JvpOcj-f49JRoe_33K7oa8yi4UC1AC_04e0rXqJrZRUSBskEHRgoOWlrdLM5qo4PERwxcmsmjkNI-tFVG-T4qOzfkbg0AegDZK-hQGx694cB7bicEpjjOVHPpPjNtdcGYrvzG0oW8Nsktte4Hk_-YGJgYzvPswjaFu2RiuGkmBoA7LBPDsVOh2noqiTY82kgJj3by21f53mu42r3kiqFS3rqlrqkpbt5PSokObZjir9VT-l2ufz3xduInAAD__zRdXzw), including increased competition from major carriers, declining fare revenues, and a failed $3.8 billion merger attempt with JetBlue that was blocked by federal regulators earlier this year. The airline [assured passengers](http://email.editor.jointheflyover.com/c/eJx00LFuhDAQBNCvMZ3Rrm0BLlykobu0KZGB9bGRwcj2Hbq_jy5Km3Kkp9Fovm7TzXPk4_7pd3I1pEmBMhJRop1Wz_E1mWZ1gUiRbshhrxVgp4eu2ZxdwJgZZjXYWS120YYwgParDQSrDw27dxsiWkRtDbbU9RB8DwgWhplQGKCVa8rtd-KjbhTiKz0pt0vam-i2Ws8i9IdQo1DjdV3tmQ-6ysWZ3kSo8R1lpki-UBFqLCdnrtJzjnxQkVzKg4pMJx0yUq2UZU3Sxyj_5P1BpRapQWkYtMF2q3tsslvPi2Pk0vXCwH33HH9H1f9Oejr1EwAA__8xrmt5) they can continue to book and fly as normal, use all tickets and loyalty points, and access Saver$ Club perks during the restructuring process, which it expects to complete in the first quarter of 2025. The bankruptcy filing will result in Spirit being delisted from the New York Stock Exchange.
## Map: Average Grocery Bill Per State
American households [spend an average of $270.21](http://email.editor.jointheflyover.com/c/eJx0kDFvgzAQhX-N2UC-s2Pw4KELW7p2RJf4HK5ycARWEP--op27vacnvU_6vq7TlSTL8vikJ4eayoQabQvQgp8iST4m28SQmJFNwwF6gxqcGVwzB0t0MQP4wQxsEtmIaG-Oz0qOvW4knG8A4AGMt9Cx63WiXoP2ergxKKs5Si1r911kqTOnfJQ3r929PJsc5lpfmzIfCkeF477vXeQs23zOCsdUSlQ4koPLxRvXKxwfa7nzerSvVe68tVulylt7O_5SK0vKVKUsCsdmDfG1S86yuV5Z_XiS5F9w_U_EO-BPAAAA__8mZl7z) per week on groceries, with Hawaii residents paying the most at $334, according to U.S. Census Bureau data. Since August 2020, [grocery prices have risen by 20%](http://email.editor.jointheflyover.com/c/eJx0z7Fu6yAUANCvgS3WvcDDMDBET_KWDo2qjhY2F5sKhwhoqvx91e79gSOd98t88Smn2_biD3I9llmAUCfEE9o5-JSfs-LBRSJBkpPDUQpALY3mu7PGSGl0tMuqZLRyMXqN_4z0gWS0hnhyPxoiWkRpFQ6kR4h-BAQLZiFkCiikXurwUdKt7xTzszyoDms5eHZ77_fG5JmJiYkpVgpD67l8phYpDKVuTEyNaqLGxPT_7foKAHA9T4i8unD_SjmnpkemYDt8yr9q_2v5cOI7AAD___jvUnI), the steepest inflation since the 1970s, with shipping costs being the primary driver. Farming and food production-based economies have kept grocery costs lower in Midwest states, while California, Washington, and New York face higher prices, reflecting overall higher living costs. Overall, 31 states spend below the average weekly grocery cost, with Wisconsin having the lowest at $221. [See where your state ranks here.](http://email.editor.jointheflyover.com/c/eJx0kD2PozAQQH-N6Yw8Yx8fhYtr6HLtlWhiD2FWBiPsBOXfr7L99k9PT-__bb6RJNkf_2hjX5c8o0GnATSMcyRJ79k10S_MyLZhD71FA50dumb1aAaORP09IuMYGGL4M1geIt2ddega8R8bAIwAdnTQctebhXoDZjTDnUE5w1FqPtuvLHtdeUnv_OKzDXlrkl9rPYqyfxVOCqfrutqXlCelQIdUSlLqB1Q4bXQcHPWaL709w6pp41MC7UWXg_eo864fZw58Chctu2YKqy6VKiucmtPH45KUpHS9cuaxkaSfgvrbkZfH7wAAAP__crZlQw)

The airline they can continue to book and fly as normal, use all tickets and loyalty points, and access Saver$ Club perks during the restructuring process, which it expects to complete in the first quarter of 2025. name: History Facts
subject: Basketball was originally played with this other ball
body:
Long before bright orange basketballs made their way onto NBA courts, the earliest basketball games were played using soccer balls. [](https://track.historyfacts.com/?xtl=1krv9cfc68bftshgf27iiphlhb0tuwk9pclb6sw6nvb0yvej36k0mat5x55bukm7vuhg92pqgl4312niq7748zigmig0jn8solg4n4ifif6ujph3xrl7wig4zrcxdyf749k3qf6quvacn90quorud1bbk&eih=kzm8nstj6prmj7bhz7sjvvnysq73bagh&__stmp=sn7660&__onlt=h&email=dpwillis67%40gmail.com)
---
---
# [Basketball was originally played with a soccer ball.](https://track.historyfacts.com/?&eih=kzm8nstj6prmj7bhz7sjvvnysq73bagh&__stmp=sn7660&__onlt=h&email=dpwillis67%40gmail.com)
### [Arts & Culture](https://track.historyfacts.com/?&eih=kzm8nstj6prmj7bhz7sjvvnysq73bagh&__stmp=sn7660&__onlt=h&email=dpwillis67%40gmail.com)
[](https://track.historyfacts.com/?&eih=kzm8nstj6prmj7bhz7sjvvnysq73bagh&__stmp=sn7660&__onlt=h&email=dpwillis67%40gmail.com)
| L ong before bright orange basketballs made their way onto NBA courts, the earliest basketball games were played using [soccer balls](https://track.historyfacts.com/?&eih=kzm8nstj6prmj7bhz7sjvvnysq73bagh&__stmp=sn7660&__onlt=h&email=dpwillis67%40gmail.com). Basketball was invented in 1891 by James Naismith, a physical education teacher at the International YMCA Training School in Springfield, Massachusetts, who sought to create a new indoor activity that could be played by students during the winter months. Naismith asked the school’s janitor to fetch him two 18-square-inch boxes for “goals,” but the janitor returned with two peach baskets instead. Naismith nailed those baskets to the railing of the gymnasium balcony, which happened to be 10 feet off the ground (this is why official NBA hoops measure 10 feet high today). After Naismith procured a soccer ball, as basketballs of course didn’t exist yet, the new game was ready to be played.
---
The original version of basketball followed a [list of 13 rules](https://track.historyfacts.com/?&eih=kzm8nstj6prmj7bhz7sjvvnysq73bagh&__stmp=sn7660&__onlt=h&email=dpwillis67%40gmail.com) regarding fouls and ball movement. Players were required to remain stationary when in possession of the ball, as continuous dribbling wasn’t instituted until 1909. The game featured nine players per side and was divided into 15-minute halves. Naismith also enlisted the help of two men to retrieve the ball from the baskets at each end, as holes weren’t cut into the bottom of the baskets until years later. The game proved extremely popular, and the first official basketball was developed by the sports equipment company Spalding in 1894. The new sport led to the creation of the National Basketball League in 1937 and the Basketball Association of America in 1946, which merged to form the National Basketball Association in 1949.
[](https://track.historyfacts.com/?&eih=kzm8nstj6prmj7bhz7sjvvnysq73bagh&__stmp=sn7660&__onlt=h&email=dpwillis67%40gmail.com) | [](https://track.historyfacts.com/?&eih=kzm8nstj6prmj7bhz7sjvvnysq73bagh&__stmp=sn7660&__onlt=h&email=dpwillis67%40gmail.com)

## Pedestrianism was a competitive sport in the 19th century.
[Pedestrianism](https://track.historyfacts.com/?&eih=kzm8nstj6prmj7bhz7sjvvnysq73bagh&__stmp=sn7660&__onlt=h&email=dpwillis67%40gmail.com) — a form of competitive walking — gained widespread popularity throughout the United States in the wake of the Civil War. As more people moved into cities, residents turned to the sport of pedestrianism to fill an activity void. The sport was simple but entertaining: Participants competed in six-day walking competitions in large-scale arenas, hoofing it several hundred miles over the course of the contest. The events often featured intense wagering, live bands, and food vendors. Many professional pedestrians also employed their own unique strides: Edward Payson Weston was renowned for his signature wobble, and Daniel O’Leary pumped his arms furiously while clutching corn cobs in each hand to absorb his sweat. Pedestrianism grew into such a spectacle that on September 21, 1879, 13 professional walkers gathered at the original Madison Square Garden in New York City to compete in front of 10,000 fans. Alas, public interest in pedestrianism began to wane by the 1890s as other organized sports such as baseball and football rose to prominence.

The sport was simple but entertaining: Participants competed in six-day walking competitions in large-scale arenas, hoofing it several hundred miles over the course of the contest. The events often featured intense wagering, live bands, and food vendors. Many professional pedestrians also employed their own unique strides: Edward Payson Weston was renowned for his signature wobble, and Daniel O’Leary pumped his arms furiously while clutching corn cobs in each hand to absorb his sweat. Pedestrianism grew into such a spectacle that on September 21, 1879, 13 professional walkers gathered at the original Madison Square Garden in New York City to compete in front of 10,000 fans. Alas, public interest in pedestrianism began to wane by the 1890s as other organized sports such as baseball and football rose to prominence. name: MassGOP
subject: MassGOP Calls for End to Illegal Teachers’ Strike by MTA
body:
| | | | |
---
| [](https://qgzxgy5ab.cc.rs6.net/tn.jsp?f=001ao27j6Cx_9LTGSp4FZDu0qhNPypn_jW_F4Z9M-tk_dq6LBWkS6ITddNv6aKLzaBRZFpPLe89wrsP2XuG8Te6b-E0Q-7Ol9qb045SQSRgaDKPx8HuTyOPtRAp2XPKAWq835rJCEcxuMNBo6SEL12G2g==&c=8gqiIBc9uraXj21AVY3kkUFYzjOGhl7BbsH48q0YtR6IgfqJy9pB8g==&ch=2PKezuZoJaBBKO70xYBLExwMWMCzCX332kKJfi6o-tnqgN8iIcXV0A==)
---
| MassGOP Calls for End to Illegal Teachers' Strike by MTA
---
---
| Boston, MA- Today marks the eighth consecutive day of an illegal teachers' strike disrupting the lives of families in Gloucester, Beverly, and Marblehead. The Massachusetts Teachers Association (MTA), the state's most powerful special interest group, is once again prioritizing its own agenda over the needs of students, parents, and communities.
In this election cycle alone, the MTA Independent Expenditure Committee has spent $245,000 to support political candidates aligned with its agenda. This extraordinary level of spending underscores the outsized influence the MTA wields in Massachusetts politics, often at the expense of families and students.
"Families are being held hostage by the MTA's reckless and illegal actions," said MassGOP Chair Amy Carnevale. "Parents are forced to pay out of pocket for childcare or miss work to stay home with their kids, all while classrooms remain empty and students lose valuable learning time. These strikes don't help teachers or families--they only serve the MTA's political objectives."
The MTA has repeatedly used extreme tactics to push its demands, often leaving students, parents, and local communities to bear the brunt of the consequences. Illegal strikes disrupt daily life, hinder children's education, and create financial and emotional stress for families across Massachusetts.
"This illegal strike is hurting the very people the MTA claims to support," added Carnevale. "Instead of engaging in good faith negotiations, the MTA is banging drums and making noise while families pay the price. It's time for this powerful special interest group to stop playing games with our children's education and work toward a solution that puts kids and families first."
The MassGOP is calling on the MTA to end this unlawful strike immediately, return educators to the classroom, and resolve contract negotiations without resorting to extreme and harmful tactics.
"The children of Gloucester, Beverly, and Marblehead deserve better. Parents deserve better. Teachers deserve better," concluded Carnevale. "It's time for the MTA to do the right thing and put students ahead of their political posturing."
###
---

name: Sign Your Name (via Left Action)
subject: Tell Morning Joe hosts to stop normalizing Trump by meeting with him and pretending he’s a normal president
body:
| | | | |
---
| | |
# [Tell Morning Joe hosts to stop normalizing Trump by meeting with him and pretending he’s a normal president](https://default.salsalabs.org/Tcf6a968e-75e3-44b6-bc1a-4ef01020a227/368d17ff-e836-4168-8049-c5f0900fbbf9)
---
| | [](https://default.salsalabs.org/T6527c6b8-f494-4769-8714-e437e7c839aa/368d17ff-e836-4168-8049-c5f0900fbbf9)
---
| | | After vociferously criticizing Trump during the campaign, and explaining how dangerous it would be to put him back in the White House, MSNBC "Morning Joe" co-hosts Mika Brzezinski and Joe Scarborough did a 180 and announced that they had flown to Mar-a-Lago to meet with Trump to "re-start communications." And then they talked about it as if it were a friendly chat with a normal president. But Trump is not a normal president. He is a wannabe fascist dictator who has threatened to jail reporters and put their companies out of business. And when journalists like Joe and Mika go to Mar-a-Lago to kiss his ring and treat him like royalty, they implicitly endorse his behavior and surrender to him before he has even taken office. This is the moment when journalists need to stick together and fight against Trump's attacks on the first amendment. It is not the time to kiss his rear end, appease him, and let him win the war without a fight. [**Add your name: Tell Morning Joe hosts to stop normalizing Trump by meeting with him and pretending he's a normal president.**](https://default.salsalabs.org/T30e7444c-eb32-49c3-8367-2b11bf8c0c86/368d17ff-e836-4168-8049-c5f0900fbbf9)

name: Heidi Ganahl
subject: The Mountain Minute: 11/19/2024
body:
Tina Peters appeal filed while justice awaits Jena Griswold
| | | | [](https://link.email.rockymountainvoice.)
---
**PRESENTED BY[ROCKYMOUNTAINVOICE.COM](https://link.email.rockymountainvoice.)**
| | Hi friends,
In today's newsletter, [Tina Peters’ appeal](https://link.email.rockymountainvoice.) moves forward as questions swirl around election integrity, while Colorado voters approve a controversial [6.5% excise tax on firearms](https://link.email.rockymountainvoice.) and ammunition. Western Colorado ranchers brace for the [next wave of wolf reintroductions](https://link.email.rockymountainvoice.), raising concerns about ecological and economic impacts.
We have some great original commentaries for you, as well. A powerful op-ed argues against [political flags in Colorado classrooms](https://link.email.rockymountainvoice.), calling for an educational environment free from partisanship. This meeting is **TODAY in Durango** , so please make sure to give this a read and take action! We also discuss the [reality of government spending](https://link.email.rockymountainvoice.), [government efficiency](https://link.email.rockymountainvoice.) (or lack thereof), and making a difference by [volunteering for local boards/committees](https://link.email.rockymountainvoice.).

This is the Mountain Minute for Tuesday, November 19, 2024.
– Heidi Ganahl
---
|
---
**TODAY'S TOP STORIES**
**[Tina Peters appeal filed while justice awaits Jena Griswold](https://link.email.rockymountainvoice.)** BY HEIDI GANAHL, [ROCKY MOUNTAIN VOICE](https://link.email.rockymountainvoice.)
[](https://link.email.rockymountainvoice.)
The legal journey of Tina Peters, former Clerk and Recorder of Mesa County, Colorado, has become emblematic of the contentious debates surrounding election integrity and judicial fairness. Peters now seeks bond pending appeal, citing significant procedural and constitutional concerns in her trial.
Peters was convicted on charges that stemmed from her decision to hire a cybersecurity expert to create forensic images of Mesa County’s election management system (EMS). This action followed a directive from the Colorado Secretary of State to perform a software update, which Peters believed might erase key election records. Her intent, she insists, was to comply with federal laws mandating the preservation of election records for 22 months.
The crux of the prosecution’s case centered on Peters’ alleged misrepresentation of the expert’s identity and breach of procedural protocols. The court sentenced her to nine years in prison, citing her vocal criticisms of election security as dangerous to the community.
**[READ MORE](https://link.email.rockymountainvoice.)**
|
---
The Rocky Mountain Voice is a 501(c)(3) that exists to promote informed civic engagement and the principles of self-governance through enterprise reporting on public policy, politics, government, and culture throughout the state of Colorado. The only way we can continue to spread truth is with your support!
[DONATE TO ROCKY MOUNTAIN VOICE](https://link.email.rockymountainvoice.)
|
---
**[Colorado voters approve Prop. KK, imposing new 6.5% excise tax on firearms and ammunition sales](https://link.email.rockymountainvoice.)** [ROCKY MOUNTAIN VOICE](https://link.email.rockymountainvoice.)
* Colorado voters approved Prop. KK, a new 6.5% excise tax on firearms, ammunition, and specific parts, expected to generate $39 million annually for public safety and mental health initiatives, starting April 2025.
* The tax applies to licensed firearm dealers but excludes general sporting goods stores; small firearm retailers expressed concerns about potential impacts on local businesses and consumer behavior.
* Funds will be allocated to crime victim support, mental health services for veterans, children’s behavioral health programs, and school security, addressing gaps left by reduced federal funding.

**[READ MORE](https://link.email.rockymountainvoice.)**
|
---
**[Western Colorado ranchers bracing for impact of the next release of wolves](https://link.email.rockymountainvoice.)** [ROCKY MOUNTAIN VOICE](https://link.email.rockymountainvoice.)
* Colorado Parks and Wildlife (CPW) is moving into phase two of its wolf reintroduction program, importing 10 to 15 gray wolves from Canada to be released in Garfield, Pitkin, and Eagle counties, despite local opposition and past failures with wolf transplants.
* The program has faced setbacks, including high costs exceeding $5 million and challenges with survival rates among transplanted wolves, as well as concerns from ranchers about livestock predation and elk herd recovery following severe winter losses.
* Critics, including wildlife professionals, argue against "ballot box biology," emphasizing that science-based wildlife management should guide conservation efforts, pointing to past failures in managing species introduced through public mandates.

**[READ MORE](https://link.email.rockymountainvoice.)**
|
---
**[Ganahl: Say ‘NO’ to political flags in our classrooms!](https://link.email.rockymountainvoice.)** BY HEIDI GANAHL, [ROCKY MOUNTAIN VOICE](https://link.email.rockymountainvoice.)
_The Durango 9-R School Board is poised to take an alarming step that undermines the values of neutrality and inclusivity our schools should uphold._
_On Tuesday, the school board plans to vote on a resolution that would officially designate certain political symbols — like the Black Lives Matter and Progress Pride flags — as the district’s own speech. This sleight of hand allows them to avoid the protections of the 1st Amendment, shutting the door on diverse perspectives while granting preferential treatment to one viewpoint._
_Let’s be clear: this is government-sanctioned discrimination. By rebranding these flags as “compatible with educational goals,” the board is imposing a specific political ideology on our classrooms. This is wrong. Our schools should be safe spaces for learning, free from divisive political messaging._
**[READ MORE](https://link.email.rockymountainvoice.)**
|
---
**[Daniel: Eventually, you run out of other people’s money](https://link.email.rockymountainvoice.)** BY BOBBIE DANIEL, [ROCKY MOUNTAIN VOICE](https://link.email.rockymountainvoice.)
_Colorado, long admired for its breathtaking landscapes, vibrant outdoor culture, and once-thriving economy, finds itself at a crossroads._
_Rising living costs, unaffordable housing, stagnant wages and increasing business closures are eroding the state’s appeal. A state once a beacon of opportunity is now grappling with mounting challenges that jeopardize its economic vitality._
_Recent data highlights the gravity of the situation. According to the Common Sense Institute (2023), Colorado’s net population growth was a mere 7,236 people, ranking 16th nationally —- a stark contrast to its historic reputation as a magnet for newcomers. The University of Colorado’s Leeds School of Business reported a troubling 22% drop in new business filings compared to the previous year. Meanwhile, states like Utah, Texas and Florida continue attracting families and businesses with lower living costs and business-friendly environments._
**[READ MORE](https://link.email.rockymountainvoice.)**
|
---
**[Madison: Efficiency in government, an oxymoron!](https://link.email.rockymountainvoice.)** BY JOHN ALEXANDER MADISON, [ROCKY MOUNTAIN VOICE](https://link.email.rockymountainvoice.)
_A U.S. senator, Chris Coons (D-DE), said again he is “amused” that it will take two billionaires to oversee President-elect Donald Trump’s new Department of Government Efficiency! (D.O.G.E)_
_He is amused that it would take two people to run this new department. But the average American patriot is hardly laughing that it takes $200 billion and 4,400 federal employees to run a useless and inefficient Department of Education. If you ask me, I’d suggest hiring two well-paid people every time we can eliminate 4,400 federal employees from our ever-expanding and bloated federal bureaucracy...one agency or department after another._
**[READ MORE](https://link.email.rockymountainvoice.)**
|
---
**[Gaines: You can make a difference by volunteering for a local board or committee](https://link.email.rockymountainvoice.)** BY CORY GAINES, [ROCKY MOUNTAIN VOICE](https://link.email.rockymountainvoice.)
_Realigning the main drag through Sterling left a couple parcels of land orphaned. It wasn’t necessarily that they looked worse than they did before — one of them was a former trailer park where the only vestiges of its former life were bare concrete pads with socketless meter boxes poking up here and there. The highway realignment didn’t make them ugly, it made them uglier: it left a bit of land whose shape wouldn’t be too conducive to anything useful. Yet another empty patch of dirt on my daily commute._ ___Then, one day, I saw some landscaping happening. It’s since been finished and has really improved the look of what otherwise would’ve been some awkwardly-shaped frontage. A city tax paid for the landscaping, but if you think that the effort was spearheaded by the mayor or City Council, you’re wrong. A citizen’s board did. A group of people working mostly behind the scenes, with a modest commitment of their time, are largely responsible for making definite improvements to their city that everyone gets to enjoy._
**[READ MORE](https://link.email.rockymountainvoice.)**
|
---
**ONLY ON ROCKY MOUNTAIN VOICE ⤵️**
**[Devotional: Embrace the strength of meekness in your bearing](https://link.email.rockymountainvoice.)** **BY DRAKE HUNTER | COMMENTARY | NOVEMBER 16** _What does it mean to have good bearing? It’s more than just your posture or how you present yourself in a room — though, that matters._
**[Colorado GOP calls voters into action against Jena Griswold’s cover-up of password leak](https://link.email.rockymountainvoice.)** **BY JEN SCHUMANN | STATE | NOVEMBER 15** _The Colorado Republican Party is attempting to rally residents of the state into action._ _Amid claims of compromised election security, party leaders are urging voters to demand accountability from officials through the filing of affidavits._
__
[**Second portrait of President-elect Trump will not be purchased for Colorado capitol**](https://link.email.rockymountainvoice.)
[****](https://link.email.rockymountainvoice.)**BY BRIAN PORTER | STATE |****NOVEMBER 15** _A second portrait of Republican President-elect Donald J. Trump will not be purchased and displayed in the Colorado capitol building, Lois Court confirmed Friday in a meeting of the Capitol Building Advisory Committee._

**Feel like you have a unique story or commentary to share?** We'd love to talk with you about becoming an RMV Contributor. Reply to this email (or reach out to [brian@rockymountainvoice.com](mailto:brian@rockymountainvoice.com?subject=Contributor%20Interest%20\(via%20Mountain%20Minute\))) or send us a message on social media to chat!
|
---
|
---
**🤔 DID YOU KNOW?** From 1776 to 1800, **the U.S. capital moved between nine cities** due to safety concerns during the Revolutionary War and a lack of centralized infrastructure. Philadelphia was the first capital after the Declaration of Independence but was abandoned multiple times due to British threats and other conflicts. The capital stabilized in Washington, D.C., in 1800, following the Residence Act, after a decade of Philadelphia serving as the temporary seat of government. [Read more here!](https://link.email.rockymountainvoice.)
|
---
**CARTOON OF THE DAY**
|
---
**QUOTE OF THE DAY**
“The mystery of government is not how Washington works but how to make it stop.” – P. J. O'Rourke

clean_body: Tina Peters appeal filed while justice awaits Jena Griswold | | | | PRESENTED BY | | Hi friends, In today's newsletter, moves forward as questions swirl around election integrity, while Colorado voters approve a controversial and ammunition. Western Colorado ranchers brace for the , raising concerns about ecological and economic impacts. We have some great original commentaries for you, as well. A powerful op-ed argues against , calling for an educational environment free from partisanship. This meeting is TODAY in Durango , so please make sure to give this a read and take action! We also discuss the , (or lack thereof), and making a difference by . This is the Mountain Minute for Tuesday, November 19, 2024. – Heidi Ganahl | TODAY'S TOP STORIES BY HEIDI GANAHL, The legal journey of Tina Peters, former Clerk and Recorder of Mesa County, Colorado, has become emblematic of the contentious debates surrounding election integrity and judicial fairness. Peters now seeks bond pending appeal, citing significant procedural and constitutional concerns in her trial. Peters was convicted on charges that stemmed from her decision to hire a cybersecurity expert to create forensic images of Mesa County’s election management system (EMS). This action followed a directive from the Colorado Secretary of State to perform a software update, which Peters believed might erase key election records. Her intent, she insists, was to comply with federal laws mandating the preservation of election records for 22 months. The crux of the prosecution’s case centered on Peters’ alleged misrepresentation of the expert’s identity and breach of procedural protocols. The court sentenced her to nine years in prison, citing her vocal criticisms of election security as dangerous to the community. | The Rocky Mountain Voice is a 501(c)(3) that exists to promote informed civic engagement and the principles of self-governance through enterprise reporting on public policy, politics, government, and culture throughout the state of Colorado. The only way we can continue to spread truth is with your support! | Colorado voters approved Prop. KK, a new 6.5% excise tax on firearms, ammunition, and specific parts, expected to generate $39 million annually for public safety and mental health initiatives, starting April 2025. The tax applies to licensed firearm dealers but excludes general sporting goods stores; small firearm retailers expressed concerns about potential impacts on local businesses and consumer behavior. Funds will be allocated to crime victim support, mental health services for veterans, children’s behavioral health programs, and school security, addressing gaps left by reduced federal funding. | Colorado Parks and Wildlife (CPW) is moving into phase two of its wolf reintroduction program, importing 10 to 15 gray wolves from Canada to be released in Garfield, Pitkin, and Eagle counties, despite local opposition and past failures with wolf transplants. The program has faced setbacks, including high costs exceeding $5 million and challenges with survival rates among transplanted wolves, as well as concerns from ranchers about livestock predation and elk herd recovery following severe winter losses. Critics, including wildlife professionals, argue against "ballot box biology," emphasizing that science-based wildlife management should guide conservation efforts, pointing to past failures in managing species introduced through public mandates. | BY HEIDI GANAHL, The Durango 9-R School Board is poised to take an alarming step that undermines the values of neutrality and inclusivity our schools should uphold. On Tuesday, the school board plans to vote on a resolution that would officially designate certain political symbols — like the Black Lives Matter and Progress Pride flags — as the district’s own speech. This sleight of hand allows them to avoid the protections of the 1st Amendment, shutting the door on diverse perspectives while granting preferential treatment to one viewpoint. Let’s be clear: this is government-sanctioned discrimination. By rebranding these flags as “compatible with educational goals,” the board is imposing a specific political ideology on our classrooms. This is wrong. Our schools should be safe spaces for learning, free from divisive political messaging. | BY BOBBIE DANIEL, Colorado, long admired for its breathtaking landscapes, vibrant outdoor culture, and once-thriving economy, finds itself at a crossroads. Rising living costs, unaffordable housing, stagnant wages and increasing business closures are eroding the state’s appeal. A state once a beacon of opportunity is now grappling with mounting challenges that jeopardize its economic vitality. Recent data highlights the gravity of the situation. According to the Common Sense Institute (2023), Colorado’s net population growth was a mere 7,236 people, ranking 16th nationally —- a stark contrast to its historic reputation as a magnet for newcomers. The University of Colorado’s Leeds School of Business reported a troubling 22% drop in new business filings compared to the previous year. Meanwhile, states like Utah, Texas and Florida continue attracting families and businesses with lower living costs and business-friendly environments. | BY JOHN ALEXANDER MADISON, A U.S. senator, Chris Coons (D-DE), said again he is “amused” that it will take two billionaires to oversee President-elect Donald Trump’s new Department of Government Efficiency! If this law gets passed and signed into law, Donald Trump’s immunity would be erased and the far-right Supreme Court’s power would instantly be limited! CAN YOU BELIEVE IT?! But we need EVERY SINGLE DEMOCRAT who reads this to endorse this bill before midnight. >> _**[[ ADD YOUR NAME → ]](https://default.salsalabs.org/T1bf11cea-e11e-473c-96c7-cbb549490ef2/368d17ff-e836-4168-8049-c5f0900fbbf9)**_

| | | Here are three quick reasons why this bill is so important: 1) Trump would be held accountable for his many crimes – including the 34 felony convictions on his record. 2) The Supreme Court, controlled by a 6-3 conservative majority, would stop any corruption that they’ve been a part of! 3) Trump would not be in office EVER AGAIN. Don’t believe us? Just take a look: [](https://default.salsalabs.org/T55a95473-406d-4437-a098-ec5af420872c/368d17ff-e836-4168-8049-c5f0900fbbf9)

| | | About Blue Amp Action: We cannot sugarcoat the current situation. Right-wing authoritarianism, defeated by a worldwide coalition during WWII, was only in hibernation. Over the past decade it has infected democratic societies--including ours. It is as simple as it is terrifying: What we do now will define the future of our country and world. We at Blue Amp Action are dedicated to liberal, open societies, where reason and science are applied to solve our problems; where civil rights--including voting rights!--are protected for all. We fight for these values every day. Speaking up on our platforms, raising the voices and the political prospects of liberal pols and pundits. **And savvy grassroots activists like you know, just like we do, that we can’t stop watching the people we help elect:_ after the elections, we have to keep organizing support for the policies we like and pushing back against the ones we don’t._** **__When the public tunes out between elections and the only people our elected officials hear from are the paid lobbyists of wealthy corporations, our government stops being accountable to the needs of ordinary families.__** **Democracy never sleeps, and neither can we.** So we ask that you support our work, so we can continue to engage in this fight every moment of every day. So our kids grow up in democracy, not dystopia. Thank you for your support for all we do! Please follow Cliff Schecter on Twitter @cliffschecter, or subscribe to The Takedown with Cliff Schecter on YouTube, to keep up to date with this battle for the heart and soul of our country. — _You're getting this message because you’ve supported good causes and Democratic candidates. We need your support to continue fighting for our democracy. name: Elizabeth Nader
subject: SHOCKING NJ data in swing right
body:
Peter, We shocked the country two weeks ago and the Left is still reeling. Is it still a little (or a lot) entertaining to wa
|
---
| Peter, We shocked the country two weeks ago and the Left is still reeling. Is it still a little (or a lot) entertaining to watch the elites melt down? Yes. Yes it is. But getting serious for a moment, we’ve been digging into the data and it just keeps getting more exciting. At a high level, Trump got closer to winning New Jersey than any Republican presidential candidate since George H.W. Bush got within 2% of Bill Clinton in 1992. As of now, the margin separating President-Elect Trump and Vice President Kamala Harris is 5.6% (with final election results to be certified by December 5). Contrast that with Biden versus Trump in 2020 (57% to 41%) and you have a **10+ point swing to the right.** Wow. Then, we went deeper into the county-by-county data. **Here are the top five New Jersey counties** with the biggest swings towards Donald Trump from 2020 to 2024.
---
|
---
| Those are BIG numbers. And they tell the story of New Jersey’s Common Sense Majority rising up electorally for the first time in a major election. We’ve seen whispers of it over the last five years (like when Ed Durr took out Steve Sweeney). But not to this scale. So that brings me to November 2025 –**350 days away** , in case you’re counting, which I am. We need to use the momentum from these results to identify, motivate and activate more common sense voters across our state. They’ve felt vilified, ignored, and silenced, but these results should give more of them the courage to come forward. Because frankly, we’ve had enough of the madness around us, and it's clear we can have an impact when we show up and vote the way we want to, not the way the elites tell us. Which is exactly what happened two weeks ago.
---
| | [JOIN the Common Sense Majority](https://xtz223.keap-link015.com/v2/click/2e375f670ddff3b61e03af5a778644f6/eJyNkMsKglAQht9l1pKp2cKdSIhoLqLW4WXIQ54LxzEy8d07VrgqCGY18803_DMCoSgEJTUEcKeH63pggcaKKYaCIimoqF5T1984nmNBy8Q11rJXEIxflxdgbnuOt7WABoUGOR7CKE3y-JwleWpIVWhz40-Pv148u32YZDBNP8XIGe1uxt1BQLrHOVHNTCo66dbwDZHqAtuuJOdSdGiqavtyJfXF5shL1F3DlG1MhVIo6s8bUhzevukJqhdmXA==)
---
| | [I’m already a member, but I’d like to chip in for 2025!](https://xtz223.keap-link015.com/v2/click/4a8d8b99aceed943ae457cb20f167b26/eJyNkE0LgkAQhv_LnDVT6-ItREIsD1Hn2NaBlnR2WUfJwv_e9oGngq4zz_sM79yBkQRxXkECV75FUQweWJTKKCRONbGQr220XIRx6EGt6LK2ujOQ3L-GJ-A5jsN44QEPBh2y363SIi_Xx01eFo40wrobf3qW88mTbVf5BsbxpxgbxVnv3C0kbDt8NqqUa8UHWzv-zGzaJAikbhpNfovUoi_r7jSz2PczqYNKk2B0KmEMUvX5Q4HDWzg-APoTZk8=)
---
| If you want to be part of this effort to grow and activate the Common Sense Majority in 2025, we urge you to join us as a [member](https://xtz223.keap-link015.com/v2/click/bdd48922599d352b652b320634ff9352/eJyNkMsKglAQht9l1pKpCeFOJEQ0F1Hr8DLkIc-F4xiZ-O4dK1wVBLOa-eYb_hmBUBSCkhoCuNPDdT2wQGPFFENBkRRUVK-p628cz7GgZeIaa9krCMavywswtz3H21pAg0KDHA9hlCZ5fM6SPDWkKrS58afHXy-e3T5MMpimn2LkjHY34-4gIN3jnKhmJhWddGv4hkh1gW1XknMpOjRVtX25kvpic-Ql6q5hyjamQikU9ecNKQ5v3_QEq79mXg==) of the Common Sense Club. _And if you’re already a member (you know who you are! we love you!)_ I'm urging you to take the next step and start identifying other common sense friends who should find a home here and inviting them to be part of this movement. Together, we’re going to say it like it is, and we’re going to save our state from Left Wing Lunacy. And let me tell you...what we’re seeing from our Leftist candidates for Governor should really light a fire under us! More on that to come... For New Jersey, Elizabeth Nader
---
|
---
| President
Common Sense Club [Share this newsletter on social media!](https://xtz223.keap-link015.com/v2/click/11e1799eded6a7f562b864fb9e8dea64/eJyNkEGPgkAMhf9LzyACcuFmjDEE5bBZz2Z2aGAUOpOZ4q4a_rvDuvG0JiY9vb5-L683YCRBXNSQww9fkySFACxKZRQSrzSxkL_bJFvEaRxAp-i0sXowkN_-PX4aJjmNk0UAfDHoLZ8fy1VZVJvDtqhK7zTC-ow3Odn8yVnvlsUWxvElGHvF67NnO8jZDjg1qpVvxXvbeX_LbFweRVL3vSaHfmQ3fM20bSLXanlS1IR0DGvBIlQUuu9JsKppOfJ4YQxS_febEi-PkPEOXllt8w==)
---
| Did you catch our previous email? If not, you can read it here now:
We know (by “we,” I mean common sense New Jerseyans, not Hollywood, coastal elites or the mainstream media) that woke extremism is one major reason the Left lost big last Tuesday night. Taxpayer funding of sex-change surgeries for prison inmates. Pushing a sexual agenda on kindergartners. Biological men beating up women in athletics. It’s this type of woke extremism that so many common sense New Jerseyans rejected on Tuesday when Trump got closer to winning New Jersey than any Republican since 1992. Why? Because common sense is that a criminal’s gender transition (or really anyone’s) shouldn’t be the financial responsibility of the taxpayer. Common sense is that children should be children and that indoctrinating them with sexually explicit materials is abusive behavior. And that women shouldn’t be losing medals and scholarships to biological men – who were usually mediocre athletes at best before deciding to compete against women. That’s the “patriarchy” the Left should be worried about. So as we look to 2025 and saving New Jersey from Left Wing Lunacy in offices from top to bottom, have candidates on the Left learned anything? Will they be moving from wokism towards common sense to, if nothing else, save their political rears? If you look at gubernatorial candidate Sean Spiller, it’s clear they won’t. Sean Spiller, the President of the New Jersey Education Association (NJEA) has been the center of some controversy for funneling NJEA resources (i.e., teacher dues) to his political campaign. But that’s just the beginning. A few days ago, “Drag Queen Entertainment” posted this video of their presence and “Banned Book Story Time” at... the NJEA Convention. This is on top of convention calls for “queering the curriculum.” The convention theme – “Learning. Equity. Justice.” should have told us all we needed to know. The message at this convention for New Jersey educators? Pushing a sexual agenda on children is not only welcomed, but encouraged. This is grooming, plain and simple. And NJ educators shouldn’t have this agenda forced on them either, especially at a time when our students are struggling and they need to be focused on core academic subjects. This is all going on under President Spiller’s lead and it has his fingerprints all over it. He has all but said he’s running to force his NJEA agenda on the entire state of New Jersey. And let’s just call this like it is – crazy, and dangerous. If the commentary from media elites and leftist insiders is any indicator, Spiller won’t be alone in continuing to push a woke agenda. But unfortunately for them, my hunch is that common sense New Jerseyans aren’t going to take it in 2025 either. So what do we do? It’s simple. 1) Identify more members of the Common Sense Majority. 2) Recruit common sense candidates for the ballot at every level. 3) Get the Common Sense Majority out to vote in the Primary and the General. It might be a simple strategy, but it’s not going to be easy. We need all of us working together to grab back that megaphone and save our state. Here at the Common Sense Club, we want you to feel like this is your home. The place where you aren’t alone in saying “that’s nuts!” and the place where you can do something about the lunacy you see around you. So as you celebrate, make plans to get back in the fight with us because 2025 is just around the corner... Let’s go, Common Sense friends! For New Jersey, Elizabeth Nader
---
|
---
| [Unsubscribe](https://xtz223.infusionsoft.com/app/optOut/0/4fd6eb57577aacb0/23717120/b3a3e5dd99a2cc9c) Common Sense Club 165 Route 35 Red Bank, New Jersey 07701 United States
---

name: Jenny Beth Martin, Tea Party Patriots Action
subject: Support Trump’s Agenda: Call Your Senators Today!
body:
Help ensure President Trump’s Cabinet gets approved – take action now!
| | | |
---
|
---
| Patriot-

---
| **** We are nearly two weeks out from Donald Trump's landslide victory across the country. But the work isn't over--we must stay active to help President Trump deliver the agenda he promised.
As you know, President Trump is already nominating people for his Cabinet, and we need you to call your senators, both Republicans and Democrats, to remind them that the American people gave a clear mandate to approve the president's choices. ___
_**CALL TO ACTION** **[Use our action page](https://qcgp-cmpzourl.maillist-manage.com/click/1edb9794a7290ff8/1edb9794a726f5de "https://www.patriotproject.com/petition/trump-nominees")** to quickly and easily be connected to your senators' offices. Tell them that the Trump agenda won at the ballot box, and approving his Cabinet is essential to honoring the American peoples' wishes.
_Why should you call Democrats?_ There are some nominees who will likely garner a few Democrat votes, so we want to start the pressure now. Additionally, with the left vowing to obstruct and resist President Trump (again), it would be good to show them that we are not unplugging because Trump won, but rather, we are staying in the fight to ensure his agenda is implemented.
As we approach Inauguration Day, the extreme left and establishment right will do everything they can to disrupt the Trump administration, but an overwhelming majority of Americans want the Trump agenda enacted. **__** **** ************
---
| | [ TAKE ACTION ](https://qcgp-cmpzourl.maillist-manage.com/click/1edb9794a7290ff8/1edb9794a726f5e0)
---
| **** ********[**Contact your senators**](https://qcgp-cmpzourl.maillist-manage.com/click/1edb9794a7290ff8/1edb9794a726f5e2) now and help get Trump's Cabinet approved!
In liberty,
Jenny Beth Martin

name: NRSC HQ
subject: Kamala ruined our borders
disclaimer_text: Paid for by the NRSC
body:
Stand with us to take back America

Friend,

Enough is enough. For too long, weak border policies from Kamala have allowed cartels to flood our communities with drugs and crime undermining our sovereignty.

**[But that stops NOW.](http://e.icyminews.com/rd/9z2zugeprnbmi3qciso9r5ab6p4hltb1vpg31qjfhd0_rp22sh2s8i6epj2c1gm8o36c1h2bs)**

Tom Homan, former ICE Director, will be joining the Trump Administration as a _**[no-nonsense leader of border control](http://e.icyminews.com/rd/9z2zk2bd3ff6thflt8ru7uetetqghgdd4hecck7ifoo_rp22sh2s8i6epj2c1gm8o36c1h2bs)**_ __**.**__ With his _AMERICA FIRST_ policies, he will restore our borders and ensure that people come here _LEGALLY. _

This isn't just about policy, it's a fight for our nation's survival. We need patriots like you, Friend to join the fight.

******************************************************[RUSH YOUR SUPPORT>>](http://e.icyminews.com/rd/9z2zjnhfrkiqm530ftvnaoi1vhrhont2ugtcec7t1vo_rp22sh2s8i6epj2c1gm8o36c1h2bs)******************************************************

#
Hi Peter,
This election outcome isn't what we wanted, but it's only a reminder of what we're fighting for: reproductive rights, Medicare and Social Security, the dignity of people, and much more.
Now is not the time to throw up our hands in despair, but to pick ourselves back up and continue our fight.
There are countless ways to hold the line, and Peter, we've built momentum like no other by organizing our communities against harmful and hateful policies, fighting Donald Trump's extremist agenda, and staying engaged to look out for one another.
And this momentum will help us in another fight we're already thinking about: **Winning back the Democratic Senate Majority in 2026.**
Once we take back the Senate, we can stop Donald Trump's most extreme policies, block his MAGA judicial nominations, and prevent Project 2025 from happening.
Election night wasn't what we expected, but the momentum we made to fight injustice everywhere started with everyday people, and our success for 2026 needs you.

subject: Liz Cheney just broke her silence
disclaimer_text: Paid for by HMP, # [#]
body:
Click here to unsubscribe. [https://secure.ngpvan.com/4xBla9HqS06_watf7h3QaQ2?source=header_rfe]
This is big.

Liz Cheney just broke her silence about the election and called on EVERY Democracy-loving American to step up.

We couldn’t agree more – so we’re asking you to help us build a pro-Democracy Majority. Please, will you rush a 900% MATCHED $15 to help us do it? >> [https://secure.actblue.com/donate/hmp-match-2024-post-ed?amount=15]

Liz Cheney: Everyone "must now be the guardrails of democracy." [https://prod.cdn.everyaction.com/images/van/NGP/NGP12/1/91305/images/20241116_em_fr_b1_hmp_main.png]

As usual, Liz Cheney is spot-on.

The LAST thing we can do after this devastating election is give up.

You deserve better.
Our country deserves better.
Our Democracy deserves better.

Stand with us to take back America

Friend,

Enough is enough. For too long, weak border policies from Kamala have allowed cartels to flood our communities with drugs and crime undermining our sovereignty.
